gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The `std.fmt` guarantee matrix (spec §4 / RFC-0016 §4.5)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " All five rows are `Exact` — `fmt` has no accuracy/precision semantics (C2). (row . tag , GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , "op '{}': every fmt row must be Exact (no accuracy semantics; C2 / spec §4)" , row . op) ; if row . op == "display_bounded" { assert ! (! row . explainable , "op '{}': only display_bounded is EXPLAIN-able" , row . op) ; assert_eq ! (row . effects , "none" , "op '{}': pure ops must declare 'none' effects (C6)" , row . op) ; } } let fallible_ops : Vec < & str > = GUARANTEE_MATRIX . iter () . filter (| r | r . fallible) . map (| r | r . op) . collect () ; assert_eq ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Project a [`Value`] to a `serde_json::Value` for `fmt`'s display wrapper, delegating the"] # [doc = " canonical projection to [`mycelium_std_io::to_json`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Refuses a non-finite `f64` with `Err(ToJsonError::NonFinite { index })` — `fmt` reports the"] # [doc = " first offending payload index for an ergonomic typed error; `std.io` independently refuses the"] # [doc = " same domain (never a silent `null`), so the seam is consistent (C1/G2)."] fn value_to_json (v : & Value) -> Result < serde_json :: Value , ToJsonError > { if let Some (index) = first_non_finite (v) { return Err (ToJsonError :: NonFinite { index }) ; } let text = mycelium_std_io :: to_json (v) . expect ("io::to_json is total over finite Values (non-finite excluded above)") ; Ok (serde_json :: from_str (& text) . expect ("io::to_json emits valid JSON, so the re-parse is total") ,) }
